documents.title: Acme Corp Q2 2024 Earnings Call
documents.content:
Operator: Good afternoon. Welcome to Acme Corp's second quarter 2024 earnings call. At this time all participants are in listen-only mode. I will now turn the call over to Jane Smith, Head of Investor Relations.

Jane Smith -- Head of IR: Thank you, operator. Joining me today are Maria Chen, CEO, and David Park, CFO. Before we begin, please note that our remarks contain forward-looking statements. Actual results may differ. We'll also discuss non-GAAP measures; reconciliations are in our 8-K filed today.

Maria Chen -- CEO: Thanks, Jane. Good afternoon, everyone. We had a strong second quarter. Revenue was $385 million, up 15% year over year and 3% sequentially. We're seeing broad-based strength across our portfolio. Enterprise continues to lead growth. We added 95 new enterprise logos in Q2, bringing total enterprise customers to 1,420. Enterprise now represents 65% of total revenue, up from 60% a year ago. International grew 20% and contributed 33% of revenue.

Maria Chen -- CEO: On guidance, we are reaffirming our full-year revenue outlook of $1.62 billion to $1.65 billion. For Q3, we expect revenue of $405 million to $420 million, representing 16% to 20% growth.

Maria Chen -- CEO: On product, Acme Platform 3.5 shipped in June with enhanced analytics and reporting capabilities. Customer feedback has been very positive. Looking ahead, we're preparing for the launch of Platform 4.0 in September, which will include our new AI Copilot feature currently in beta with 50 design partners. We're investing approximately $50 million this year in AI infrastructure and talent. We believe AI-native capabilities will be a meaningful differentiator heading into 2025.

Maria Chen -- CEO: On go-to-market, we added 35 sales reps in Q2, focused on EMEA. We now have 180 quota-carrying reps globally. Win rates in competitive deals improved to 58%, up from 55% in Q1, driven by our unified platform story.

David Park -- CFO: Thank you, Maria. Let me walk through the financial details. Non-GAAP operating margin for Q2 was 22%, up 150 basis points from a year ago. Gross margin was 77%, up 50 basis points sequentially. We're maintaining our full-year operating margin guidance of 22% to 23%.

David Park -- CFO: Free cash flow was $75 million in Q2. We ended the quarter with $1.1 billion in cash and equivalents. CapEx was $28 million, primarily data center and cloud infrastructure. We repurchased $20 million of stock in Q2. DSO was 45 days, down from 50 a year ago.

David Park -- CFO: On the M&A front, we closed the acquisition of DataFlow Labs in May for $45 million. DataFlow's real-time data pipeline technology is being integrated into Platform 4.0. We expect the acquisition to be accretive to non-GAAP EPS by Q1 2025.

David Park -- CFO: Headcount at quarter-end was 2,200 employees, up 10% year over year. R&D was 23% of revenue. Sales and marketing was 29%. G&A was 8%.

Operator: We will now begin the question-and-answer session.

Operator: The first question comes from Alex Rivera with Bernstein.

Alex Rivera -- Bernstein: Thanks. Maria, on the AI Copilot beta, can you give us any early data on engagement or willingness to pay?

Maria Chen -- CEO: Sure, Alex. We have 50 design partners testing it now. The engagement metrics are encouraging—beta users are spending 30% more time on the platform. On pricing, we're testing a 15% to 20% premium for the AI tier. No final decision yet; we'll set pricing at GA in September.

Alex Rivera -- Bernstein: And how should we think about the DataFlow integration timeline?

David Park -- CFO: The engineering integration is on track. We expect DataFlow's real-time pipeline to be fully embedded in Platform 4.0 at launch. The team has been great—no attrition from the acquisition. We retained all 60 DataFlow employees.

Operator: The next question comes from Sam Lee with Morgan Stanley.

Sam Lee -- Morgan Stanley: Hi, thanks. David, can you talk about the gross margin trajectory? 77% is solid but flat-ish. What levers do you have?

David Park -- CFO: We see three levers: first, the mix shift toward enterprise, which carries higher margins. Second, infrastructure efficiency—we're renegotiating cloud contracts and optimizing workloads. Third, pricing on renewals. We believe 78% to 79% is achievable over the next two to three quarters.

Sam Lee -- Morgan Stanley: And on the competitive landscape, are you seeing any new entrants?

Maria Chen -- CEO: The competitive set is largely the same—legacy vendors and some cloud-native startups. The hyperscalers are starting to build adjacent capabilities but haven't directly competed with us yet. Our win rate improvement to 58% suggests our positioning is strong.

Operator: The next question comes from Priya Patel with Goldman Sachs.

Priya Patel -- Goldman Sachs: Thanks. Maria, international at 33% of revenue and growing 20%—can you break that down by region?

Maria Chen -- CEO: Sure. EMEA was up 25%, driven by financial services wins in the UK and Germany. APAC ex-China was up 18%. China was roughly flat year over year at about $32 million, or 8% of revenue. We're being cautious on China given the macro environment. Latin America is small but growing fast, up 35% off a low base.

Priya Patel -- Goldman Sachs: And on the sales rep adds, are you on track for your hiring plan?

Maria Chen -- CEO: Yes. We planned to add about 80 reps this year. With 35 in Q2 and 25 in Q1, we've added 60 through the first half. We expect to add 20 to 25 more across Q3 and Q4, primarily in EMEA and APAC.

Operator: The next question comes from Tom Wilson with RBC.

Tom Wilson -- RBC: Quick one on capital allocation. You repurchased $20 million and spent $45 million on DataFlow. How do you balance M&A versus buybacks going forward?

David Park -- CFO: Our priority order is: invest in organic growth, pursue strategic tuck-in M&A, and return capital via buybacks. We don't have a formal buyback program. With $1.1 billion in cash, we have ample flexibility. The pipeline has a few interesting targets in AI and security, but nothing imminent.

Tom Wilson -- RBC: And any update on the patent litigation with CompetitorX?

Maria Chen -- CEO: We filed our response in June. The case is in early discovery. We're confident in our IP position. No material financial impact expected. We've set aside a modest legal reserve but don't expect it to be material.

Jane Smith -- Head of IR: Thanks, everyone. That concludes our Q2 call. We'll be at the Needham conference in August and the Goldman TMT conference in September. Have a good evening.

Operator: This concludes Acme Corp's second quarter 2024 earnings call. You may now disconnect.
